group.label: General Settings
group.comment: Enable Core Web Vitals monitoring to track LCP, FID/INP, and CLS metrics using the browser's native PerformanceObserver API.

field.label: Enable Module
field.comment: Master switch. When enabled, injects a lightweight JavaScript snippet on every frontend page that monitors Core Web Vitals using the PerformanceObserver API.

field.label: Debug Mode
field.comment: When enabled, all collected metrics (LCP, FID, INP, CLS) are logged to the browser's developer console. Also exposes window.coreWebVitalsMetrics for inspection. Disable in production.

field.label: Send Metrics to Analytics
field.comment: When enabled, automatically sends collected metrics to Google Analytics 4 (if gtag is present) or to a custom endpoint set via window.panthCoreWebVitalsEndpoint using navigator.sendBeacon.

group.label: LCP (Largest Contentful Paint) Monitoring
group.comment: LCP measures the time it takes for the largest visible element (image, text block, video) to render. Google considers < 2500ms "good".

field.label: Monitor LCP
field.comment: Track Largest Contentful Paint using the largest-contentful-paint PerformanceObserver entry type.

field.label: Target LCP (milliseconds)
field.comment: Your target LCP time. Used to rate metrics as "good", "needs improvement", or "poor" in console output and analytics events. Google recommends < 2500ms.

group.label: FID / INP (Interaction Responsiveness) Monitoring
group.comment: FID measures the delay before the browser processes the first user interaction. INP measures the worst interaction latency throughout the page lifecycle. INP replaced FID as a Core Web Vital in March 2024.

field.label: Monitor FID and INP
field.comment: Track First Input Delay (first-input entry type) and Interaction to Next Paint (event entry type with durationThreshold).

field.label: Target FID (milliseconds)
field.comment: Your target First Input Delay. Google recommends < 100ms.

field.label: Target INP (milliseconds)
field.comment: Your target Interaction to Next Paint. Google recommends < 200ms. INP is the primary interaction metric since March 2024.

group.label: CLS (Cumulative Layout Shift) Monitoring
group.comment: CLS measures visual stability — how much the page content shifts unexpectedly during loading. Google considers < 0.1 "good".

field.label: Monitor CLS
field.comment: Track Cumulative Layout Shift using the layout-shift PerformanceObserver entry type. Only counts shifts without recent user input.

field.label: Target CLS Score
field.comment: Your target CLS score. This is a unitless value. Google recommends < 0.1.

group.label: Resource Hints
group.comment: Resource hints tell the browser to resolve DNS, establish connections, or fetch resources ahead of time. This reduces latency for external origins like CDNs, font providers, and analytics services.

field.label: DNS Prefetch Domains
field.comment: One domain per line, without protocol (e.g., fonts.googleapis.com). Outputs <link rel="dns-prefetch"> tags in the page head. DNS prefetch resolves the domain name early, saving 20-120ms per domain on first request.

field.label: Preconnect Domains
field.comment: One domain per line, without protocol (e.g., fonts.gstatic.com). Outputs <link rel="preconnect" crossorigin> tags. Preconnect performs DNS + TCP + TLS handshake early. Use sparingly — limit to 2-4 critical origins.

field.label: Prefetch URLs
field.comment: One full URL per line (e.g., https://cdn.example.com/critical.js). Outputs <link rel="prefetch"> tags. Prefetch downloads resources at low priority for use on the next navigation. Do not use for resources needed on the current page.
